title: How Effective are Public Policies to Increase Health Insurance Coverage Among Young Adults?

construct_vars: df <- df %>% dplyr::left_join(gvar_tbl, by='stfips') %>% dplyr::mutate(gvar_CS = ifelse(!is.na(first_law), as.numeric(first_law), 0))

notes: Levine, McKnight & Heep (2011). Table 5 Panel A Row 1 Col 2 (DD Full Sample). Stata: xi: reg insured law i.stfips i.year i.a_age married student female ur povratio povratio2 if a_age>=19 & a_age<=24 & year>=2000, cluster(stfips). UNWEIGHTED (paper footnote 23). Outcome: insured = privhi|pubhi (any insurance). Treatment = state parental coverage law (staggered, 20 states treated 2000-2008). Massachusetts excluded. Target: -0.0005 (SE 0.007). N=127106. NOTE: Use original event study (with binning) as point estimate. Sensitivity (CS-NT/CS-NYT +/- married+student+female) reserved for binning chapter discussion. EVENT STUDY REVIEWED: All 5 estimators (TWFE, CS-NT, CS-NYT, SA, Gardner) show null results — estimates bounce ±0.01-0.02, all CIs include zero at every horizon. No pre-trends, no effect. The +1865% ratio is meaningless (both ≈0).
